---
title: "GenomicDistributions Drop-in Compatibility"
subtitle: "Verify calc* wrappers work without explicit RegionSet usage"
output:
  html_document:
    toc: true
    theme: flatly
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE, comment = "#>")
data_dir <- normalizePath(file.path("..", "..", "tests", "data"))
```

# Overview

This Rmd verifies that existing GenomicDistributions workflows work as a
drop-in replacement by swapping `library(GenomicDistributions)` for
`library(gtars)`. No `RegionSet()` calls — just GRanges in, results out.

```{r load}
library(gtars)
library(GenomicRanges)
```

# Single-query functions

## calcWidth

```{r calcWidth}
gr <- GRanges(
  seqnames = c("chr1", "chr1", "chr1"),
  ranges = IRanges(start = c(3, 5, 8), end = c(10, 15, 20))
)
calcWidth(gr)
```

## calcNeighborDist

```{r calcNeighborDist}
calcNeighborDist(gr)
```

## calcNearestNeighbors

```{r calcNearestNeighbors}
calcNearestNeighbors(gr)
```

# Multi-object functions

## calcPartitions with GTF

```{r calcPartitions-gtf}
gtf_path <- file.path(data_dir, "regionset", "test_gene_model.gtf")
pl <- partitionListFromGTF(gtf_path)
calcPartitions(gr, pl)
```

## calcPartitions with GRanges gene model

```{r calcPartitions-granges}
genes_gr <- GRanges(
  seqnames = c("chr1", "chr1"),
  ranges = IRanges(start = c(1, 100), end = c(50, 200)),
  strand = c("+", "-")
)
exons_gr <- GRanges(
  seqnames = c("chr1", "chr1"),
  ranges = IRanges(start = c(5, 110), end = c(20, 150)),
  strand = c("+", "-")
)
pl_gr <- genomePartitionList(genes_gr, exons_gr)
calcPartitions(gr, pl_gr)
```

## calcExpectedPartitions

```{r calcExpectedPartitions}
genome_size <- c(chr1 = 249250621L)
calcExpectedPartitions(gr, pl, genomeSize = genome_size)
```

## calcGCContent

```{r calcGCContent}
fasta_path <- file.path(data_dir, "fasta", "base.fa")
assembly <- load_genome_assembly(fasta_path)

# base.fa has chr1 (4bp) and chrX (8bp)
gr_small <- GRanges(
  seqnames = c("chr1", "chrX"),
  ranges = IRanges(start = c(1, 1), end = c(4, 8))
)
calcGCContent(gr_small, assembly)
```

## calcDinuclFreq

```{r calcDinuclFreq}
calcDinuclFreq(gr_small, assembly)
```

## calcTSSDist

```{r calcTSSDist}
tss_gr <- GRanges(
  seqnames = c("chr1", "chr1"),
  ranges = IRanges(start = c(1, 50), end = c(2, 51))
)
calcTSSDist(gr, tss_gr)
```

## calcFeatureDist

```{r calcFeatureDist}
calcFeatureDist(gr, tss_gr)
```

# Summary

All `calc*` functions work with GRanges input — no `RegionSet()` needed.
Existing GenomicDistributions scripts work by changing one line:

```r
# Before:
library(GenomicDistributions)

# After:
library(gtars)
```
